perturbation.original_text: The term, “Confidential Information”, does not include, and the Company has no obligation to protect information which is (i) known to or acquired by the Company before disclosure under this Agreement; (ii) independently developed by the Company without relying on ABRH’s Confidential Information; (iii) or becomes part of the public domain (other than as a result of disclosure by the Company or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement) or lawfully obtained from a third party who is not, to the Company’s knowledge, under an obligation of confidentiality to ABRH with respect to such information; (iv) free of confidentiality restrictions by written agreement of ABRH; or (v) required to be disclosed by any law, government regulation, or judicial or other governmental order, provided that, if legally permissible, the Company provides reasonable advance written notice to ABRH to afford ABRH the opportunity to seek a protective order or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term, “Confidential Information”, includes all information, regardless of prior knowledge or independent development, and the Company has an absolute obligation to protect all information received, even if known prior to disclosure under this Agreement or independently developed by the Company without relying on ABRH’s Confidential Information. (iii) or becomes part of the public domain (other than as a result of disclosure by the Company or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement) or lawfully obtained from a third party who is not, to the Company’s knowledge, under an obligation of confidentiality to ABRH with respect to such information; (iv) free of confidentiality restrictions by written agreement of ABRH; or (v) required to be disclosed by any law, government regulation, or judicial or other governmental order, provided that, if legally permissible, the Company provides reasonable advance written notice to ABRH to afford ABRH the opportunity to seek a protective order or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4(a) originally excludes certain information from the definition of 'Confidential Information.' By replacing it with 'includes all information, regardless of prior knowledge or independent development,' the clause now contradicts itself. The original text had exceptions for what is not considered confidential. This insertion creates a scenario where information known beforehand or independently developed is now also classified as confidential, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Exclusions . The term, “Confidential Information”, includes all information, regardless of prior knowledge or independent development, and the Company has an absolute obligation to protect all information received, even if known prior to disclosure under this Agreement or independently developed by the Company without relying on ABRH’s Confidential Information. (iii) or becomes part of the public domain (other than as a result of disclosure by the Company or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement) or lawfully obtained from a third party who is not, to the Company’s knowledge, under an obligation of confidentiality to ABRH with respect to such information; (iv) free of confidentiality restrictions by written agreement of ABRH; or (v) required to be disclosed by any law, government regulation, or judicial or other governmental order, provided that, if legally permissible, the Company provides reasonable advance written notice to ABRH to afford ABRH the opportunity to seek a protective order or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. If the Company becomes legally obligated or receives a subpoena or other legal demand issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or governmental body to disclose any Confidential Information, it shall cooperate with ABRH in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy, and shall use reasonable efforts to protect the confidential and proprietary status of any disclosed Confidential Information. ABRH will reimburse the Company and its Representatives for reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with its compliance with the immediately preceding sentence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'Confidential Information.' The original text excludes specific types of information (e.g., previously known information, independently developed information) from being considered confidential. The changed text explicitly includes 'all information, regardless of prior knowledge or independent development,' which directly contradicts the original exclusions and creates an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text (a) Exclusions is located in the same location as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Upon request of ABRH for any reason, the Company shall promptly return to ABRH, or at Company’s option, destroy (except originals, equipment, and devices delivered by ABRH to the Company, which shall be returned to ABRH), all equipment, documents, devices of any kind, or other material of any kind, in any form, containing any Confidential Information, together with all copies, summaries, abstracts, excerpts, extracts, replicas, reproductions, and samples of any of the foregoing, and certify the destruction of any copy or partial copy made.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon request of ABRH for any reason, the Company may, at its sole discretion, choose whether or not to return to ABRH, or at Company’s option, destroy (except originals, equipment, and devices delivered by ABRH to the Company, which shall be returned to ABRH), all equipment, documents, devices of any kind, or other material of any kind, in any form, containing any Confidential Information, together with all copies, summaries, abstracts, excerpts, extracts, replicas, reproductions, and samples of any of the foregoing, and certify the destruction of any copy or partial copy made.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates that the Company *shall* return or destroy Confidential Information upon ABRH's request. By introducing 'may, at its sole discretion, choose whether or not to return,' the clause now permits the Company to decide whether or not to comply with the request. This creates an in-text contradiction about the obligation to return or destroy the confidential information, as at first it was a must and now its optional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials . Confidential Information may be reproduced by the Company and Company Representatives for any purpose whatsoever, including but not limited to evaluating the Potential Transaction. Upon request of ABRH for any reason, the Company may, at its sole discretion, choose whether or not to return to ABRH, or at Company’s option, destroy (except originals, equipment, and devices delivered by ABRH to the Company, which shall be returned to ABRH), all equipment, documents, devices of any kind, or other material of any kind, in any form, containing any Confidential Information, together with all copies, summaries, abstracts, excerpts, extracts, replicas, reproductions, and samples of any of the foregoing, and certify the destruction of any copy or partial copy made. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Confidential Information that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and not for any other use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Company to return or destroy Confidential Information upon request, while the changed text gives the Company the discretion to choose whether or not to comply with the request. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, which means there is an in-text contradiction. Because the changed text is now optional, this directly conflicts with the original mandatory obligation, leading to an in-text contradiction as defined under 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements, proposals, inquiries, commitments, discussions, and correspondences, whether written or oral.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties except for certain side agreements, proposals, inquiries, commitments, discussions, and correspondences, whether written or oral, which shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause establishes that the Agreement is the 'entire agreement' and supersedes all prior agreements. The added exception contradicts this by stating that certain other agreements remain in effect. This creates direct contradiction and uncertainty about which agreements govern the relationship, in reference to those side agreements. It initially says the agreement supersedes prior agreements, it is now saying some of those agreements are still in effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Modification and Waiver . This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties except for certain side agreements, proposals, inquiries, commitments, discussions, and correspondences, whether written or oral, which shall remain in full force and effect. No modification to this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by a duly authorized representative of each party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless signed by the waiving party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the 'entire agreement' clause, stating that certain side agreements remain in effect. The contradicted text, which is the same clause located elsewhere, now conflicts with the changed text because one instance of the clause states the agreement is 'entire' while the other makes an exception. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as a clause placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about which agreements govern the relationship.
